title: Karachi Style Fish Fry

seo.title: How to Make Karachi Style Fish Fry — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Karachi Style Fish Fry. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Karachi Style Fish Fry
easy Karachi Style Fish Fry recipe
authentic Pakistani Karachi Style Fish Fry
lowCompetitionKeywords: Pakistani Karachi Style Fish Fry recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Karachi Style Fish Fry Recipe
h2Headings: What is Karachi Style Fish Fry?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Pakistani Karachi Style Fish Fry recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Karachi Style Fish Fry — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Karachi Style Fish Fry is a beloved Pakistani street food, known for its incredibly crispy exterior and succulent, spiced interior. This dish features marinated fish, deep-fried to golden perfection, offering a burst of traditional flavors with every bite. It is a true culinary gem from the vibrant city of Karachi.

ingredients.item: White fish fillets (e.g., Tilapia, Cod, Kingfish)

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Wash the fish pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Ensure there is no excess moisture, as this helps the marinade adhere better.

instructions.title: Marinate the Fish
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, carom seeds, lemon juice, and salt. Add the dry fish pieces to this mixture and toss gently to ensure each piece is evenly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 2 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together the gram flour, rice flour, and a pinch of salt. Gradually add water, a tablespoon at a time, mixing until you achieve a thick, smooth batter that coats the back of a spoon. The consistency should be thick enough to cling to the fish, but not overly pasty.

instructions.title: Coat the Fish
instructions.text: Dip each marinated fish piece into the prepared gram flour batter, ensuring it is fully coated. Allow any excess batter to drip off before placing it on a plate.

instructions.title: Heat the Oil
instructions.text: Heat the cooking oil in a deep frying pan or wok over medium-high heat. To test if the oil is ready, drop a tiny bit of batter; it should sizzle and float to the surface immediately. The oil should be hot enough to fry without burning, around 350-375°F (175-190°C).

instructions.title: Deep Fry the Fish
instructions.text: Carefully place the coated fish pieces into the hot oil, frying in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan. Fry for 4-6 minutes on each side, or until the fish is golden brown, crispy, and cooked through. Remove the fish with a slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack lined with paper towels to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Serve the Karachi Style Fish Fry immediately with a sprinkle of chaat masala, fresh lemon wedges, and a side of mint chutney or raita. It is best enjoyed hot and fresh.

tips: Always ensure your fish is completely dry before marinating; this helps the spices penetrate better and prevents the final product from being soggy.
For an extra layer of flavor, add a pinch of dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) to your marinade. It imparts a distinctive aroma often found in traditional Pakistani dishes.
To achieve maximum crispiness, incorporate a tablespoon of cornstarch into your gram flour batter. Also, avoid overcrowding the frying pan to maintain oil temperature and ensure even cooking.
article: Karachi Style Fish Fry is a beloved Pakistani Seafood dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
